fingerprint: The '10th harmonic' claim (117 Hz) can only work with the Tesla frequency (11.79 Hz) as its base — and that frequency is globe-derived via disc_diameter = 2πR_Earth. The Schumann fundamental (7.83 Hz × 10 = 78.3 Hz) fails by a factor of 1.49. No dome cavity resonance (quarter-wave or half-wave with firmament height) yields 11.7 Hz. The only coherent interpretation of '10th harmonic of dome resonance' requires a base frequency that is constructed from π × R_Earth — a quantity with no geometric origin on a flat disc.
globe_value: Tesla frequency f = v_Tesla / disc_diameter = 1.574c / (2 × 20,015 km) = 1.574c / (2πR_Earth) ≈ 11.79 Hz
dome_value: 117 Hz = '10th harmonic of dome resonance' (base frequency never specified by dome model)
match_precision: 117.9 Hz vs 117.0 Hz (0.8% — within Reid's measurement uncertainty)
why_it_matters: This is a transitive globe fingerprint: WIN-003's harmonic claim depends on WIN-001's Tesla frequency, which depends on the disc diameter equaling the globe's circumference (2πR_Earth = 40,030 km, match to <1 km). On a flat disc, there is no geometric reason the disc radius would equal π × R_anything. The factor π arises from the circumference of a sphere — it appears in the dome model's coordinate system because that system is an azimuthal equidistant projection of a globe. The '10th harmonic' cannot be derived from dome geometry alone; it requires borrowing the globe's circumference through WIN-001.
conditional_note: This fingerprint is CONDITIONAL on the dome's undefined base frequency being 11.79 Hz (Tesla). The dome model says 'consistent with dome resonance harmonic structure' without specifying the base. If they intended Schumann (7.83 Hz), the claim fails numerically (78.3 ≠ 117). If they intended the quarter-wave firmament resonance (8.25 Hz), it also fails (82.5 ≠ 117). The Tesla frequency is the ONLY dome-related frequency that works — and it's globe-derived.
cross_references: WIN-002 (Schumann resonance: dome cavity predicts ~22 Hz, not 7.83 Hz)
suggested_text: The dome never specifies which 'dome resonance' frequency × 10 yields 117 Hz. The Schumann fundamental (7.83 Hz × 10 = 78.3 Hz) fails by 49%. The only dome frequency that works is the Tesla frequency from WIN-001 (11.79 Hz × 10 = 117.9 Hz) — but that frequency is itself globe-derived: f = 1.574c / (2πR<sub>Earth</sub>), where the disc diameter equals the globe's circumference by construction. The '10th harmonic' claim thus inherits WIN-001's globe fingerprint through transitive dependence on π × R<sub>Earth</sub>.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence — the fingerprint is real but conditional on interpreting the undefined base frequency as 11.79 Hz (Tesla). The dome model's vagueness ('consistent with dome resonance harmonic structure') means they never commit to a specific base, making this an inference about their most charitable interpretation.
